repository.head: 8ef5b9c adds the shared numeric SuperWatch Y axis after variable-directory visibility controls and waveform workspace expansion; the final documentation commit contains this memory
repository.remote_head: the implementation and final handoff commits are pushed together on feature/online-flash-streaming
repository.working_tree: clean after the final documentation commit; generated installers, screenshots, logs, caches, dependencies, and build outputs removed

mission.product: MKLink 专用嵌入式上位机
mission.online_flash: 仅支持 MKLink 暴露的 CMSIS-DAP；支持 BIN 与 HEX；CMSIS-Pack 索引完整、DFP 按需下载到用户缓存。
mission.offline_flash: 脱机烧录是与在线烧录并列的顶级页面；支持有序多固件、多 FLM、BIN 基地址、HEX 文件内地址、自动次数、IDCODE 超时、SWD 速率、脚本预览和 MKLink U 盘部署。
mission.streaming: SystemView、VOFA、RTT 和 SuperWatch 以经过验证的稳定负载采集，目标尽量达到或超过 10 kSamples/s；仅 RTT 建立了实测过载边界；可见曲线统一限制为 30 FPS。

current_session.active_plan: none; SuperWatch waveform visibility, expanded workspace, shared numeric Y axis, installed HIL, and final regression are complete
current_session.current_task: SuperWatch now uses the variable directory for acquisition and curve visibility, gives the waveform the full right workspace, and exposes numeric shared-axis navigation; collect follow-up feedback and reproduce the remaining first-load offline target-search transient
current_session.latest_commit: 8ef5b9c feat: add shared SuperWatch Y axis
current_session.online_flash_discovery_implementation: complete; the packaged sidecar includes pyOCD and cmsis_pack_manager runtime data, the API maps enumeration failures to an actionable privacy-safe error, and page-entry discovery retries both empty results and transient cold-start failures before selecting the first matching MicroKeen probe. Selecting HEX starts inspection automatically. Selecting BIN requires an explicit valid base address and then starts inspection automatically. The manual inspect button was removed.
current_session.superwatch_symbol_workspace_implementati: complete; AXF symbols load immediately into a shared runtime-safe catalog, SuperWatch offers searchable selection and typed inline writes, and reparse preserves selected names. The redundant right Watch panel is removed, so the waveform fills the right workspace. Variable checkboxes control acquisition while adjacent eye controls independently hide or restore curves without stopping sampling or current-value updates. The waveform has a shared numeric Y axis with wheel zoom, drag pan, and double-click reset, plus the existing independent X-axis navigation. Typed writes stop continuous dump acquisition, use flush_memory, verify with command-mode readback, and restore the prior running or paused state. Reconnecting rebinds the existing runtime to the current Device instead of retaining a closed Device object.
current_session.desktop_usability_implementation: complete; Computer Use exercised connection, RTT, RTOS Trace, memory, symbols, SuperWatch, navigation, pause/resume/stop, and resource recovery before its capture channel failed. Real Edge/Playwright continued visual testing. SuperWatch stop no longer opens an English blocking confirmation, stop HTTP failures are visible, invalid symbols use a persistent Chinese inline error instead of alert(), and offline structured PROBE_BUSY details identify the owning dashboard and recovery action. A rebuilt unsigned NSIS installer was installed and the installed application passed the affected flows with Computer Use.
current_session.rtt_dashboard_implementation: complete; RTT reads now deliver at 50 Hz with a 10 ms bridge polling quantum, partial numeric batches flush every read cycle, and the virtual log coalesces near 30 FPS instead of 10 FPS. Numeric channel layouts require two consecutive matching rows so attaching mid-line cannot lock a truncated name. The demonstration firmware emits changing temp/speed values. Real Edge HIL confirmed named curves, 23.8 Canvas FPS, about 25 ms log update intervals, pause/resume behavior, and zero transport/backend loss.
current_session.offline_download_implementation: complete; top-level GUI, structured API/core, transactional U-disk deployment, model-specific filenames, trigger resource coordination, transient version retry, reuse of an already connected Device bridge in installed runtime, automated regression, and V4.3.4 dual-HEX HIL all pass
current_session.task_3_implementation: complete; final spec and quality reviews APPROVED
current_session.task_4_implementation: complete; final spec and quality reviews APPROVED
current_session.task_5_implementation: complete; final spec and quality reviews APPROVED
current_session.task_6_implementation: complete; final spec and quality reviews APPROVED
current_session.task_7_implementation: complete; final spec and quality reviews APPROVED
current_session.task_8_implementation: complete; final spec and quality reviews APPROVED
current_session.task_9_implementation: complete; final spec and quality re-reviews APPROVED; strict Tauri evidence, final dist, verification docs, and AI memory committed and pushed
current_session.task_10_implementation: complete and independently approved; all four ten-minute measurements and regenerated targetVerified/targetReset cleanup artifacts pass; SystemView Context corruption, Flash readback, and real MCU reset evidence gaps are fixed
current_session.task_11_implementation: complete; final MSI/NSIS bundles rebuilt from 56f1df6, final NSIS installed-GUI verify and VERIFY_FAIL mismatch-address HIL pass, both restricted-PATH installer lifecycles pass, and Pack/user caches are preserved
current_session.task_12_implementation: complete; 21 local release payloads and 18 sanitized JSON artifacts validate, SHA256SUMS is name-sorted, and final specification and quality reviews are APPROVED with no remaining findings
current_session.task_13_implementation: complete; 1c7c5aa tagged as v0.1.0-rc.1, GitHub prerelease published with 23 assets, every asset downloaded and SHA-256 verified, and local branch/tag/release state checked
current_session.release_qualification: Complete. The reviewed release commit is 1c7c5aac3f49f95d4195a0f07eed51bdaf6dcde6, the annotated tag and manifest identify that commit, and the GitHub prerelease is public and verified.
current_session.pause_requested: Resumed by user; the prior pause checkpoint is closed.
current_session.stop_rule: Continue autonomously through planning, testing, packaging, review, commit, push, tag, and GitHub prerelease. Stop only for irreplaceable credentials, required manual hardware action, or an unauthorized irreversible external operation.

milestones.name: Online flash Tasks 1-12
milestones.review: final spec and quality APPROVED

milestones.name: High-throughput Task 1 binary protocol
milestones.review: spec and quality APPROVED

milestones.name: High-throughput Task 2 bounded StreamHub
milestones.review: spec and quality APPROVED
milestones.notes: Unique owner loop, immutable batch, generation/pending callback lifecycle, stale callback isolation.

milestones.name: High-throughput Task 3 authenticated binary WebSocket
milestones.tests: Task 1-3 focused 64 passed; stream API 24 passed x5; full Python 452 passed
milestones.review: final spec and quality APPROVED
milestones.notes: Invalid JSON shapes and binary authentication frames close 1008 without leaks; sequence, item_count, and timestamp_ns are shared per fan-out batch.

milestones.name: High-throughput Task 4 Worker-owned stream buffers
milestones.tests: Task 4 focused 21 passed; GUI full 98 passed; Vite build 134 modules
milestones.review: final spec and quality APPROVED
milestones.notes: Fixed-capacity typed ring, transferable Worker frames, bounded reconnect lifecycle, and generation/ticket readiness acknowledgements isolate stale sockets.

milestones.name: High-throughput Task 5 envelope, scheduler, and benchmark
milestones.tests: Benchmark 14 passed; envelope/scheduler 11 passed; GUI full 109 passed; Python stream 78 passed
milestones.review: final spec and quality APPROVED
milestones.notes: Typed min/max envelope, one 30 FPS scheduler, strict sequence/drop accounting, responsive cancellation, and deterministic sample-rate benchmark.

milestones.name: High-throughput Task 6 SystemView binary migration
milestones.tests: Python full 507 passed; GUI full 153 passed; Vite build 137 modules; final focused frontend 52 and backend 41 passed
milestones.review: final spec and quality APPROVED
milestones.notes: Recording-first fixed 48-byte events, bounded O(1) Worker rings, nested ISR contexts, exact BigInt ticks, pixel-bounded interval envelopes, binary UI at 30 FPS and event table at 5 Hz.

milestones.name: High-throughput Task 7 VOFA binary migration
milestones.tests: Python full 544 passed; GUI full 192 passed; Vite build 137 modules; final focused Python 37 and GUI 88 passed
milestones.review: final spec and quality APPROVED
milestones.notes: Validated aligned source reads, atomic sample-major batches, Worker min/max envelopes, O(1) extrema and O(log N) interactions, bounded DOM work, live telemetry, pause-safe rates, and SuperWatch legacy isolation.

milestones.name: High-throughput Task 8 RTT and SuperWatch binary migration
milestones.tests: Python full 570 passed; GUI full 217 passed; Vite build 140 modules; final focused backend 93 passed
milestones.review: final spec and quality APPROVED
milestones.notes: RTT raw and numeric binary records, bounded virtual log, SuperWatch versioned metadata and sample generations, nonblocking hardware reads, late-subscriber metadata replay, and transactional dashboard cancellation/rollback without probe lease leaks.

milestones.name: High-throughput Task 9 performance and HIL qualification
milestones.tests: Python full 583 passed in 26.36s; GUI full 231 passed; Vite build 140 modules; browser/package harness 13 passed
milestones.review: final spec and quality re-reviews APPROVED
milestones.notes: All four 30-minute backend HIL gates passed at qualified stable rates. Only RTT retained a measured overload boundary; VOFA/SuperWatch used the fastest supported request and SystemView used pairs=2/tick. Real Edge visible/pause/resume gates passed with <=30 FPS and zero measured loss; hidden state was not established. Strict packaged Tauri RTT ran 300.027 seconds with exact WebSocket/Worker frame and sequence parity, zero loss, complete cleanup, and CDP browser release. Deliverable commit cc8282f is pushed.

milestones.name: Release qualification Task 10 packaged stream gates

milestones.notes: Formal packaged measurements: RTT 26.090 kHz, SystemView 20.091 kEvents/s, VOFA 8.037 kHz, SuperWatch 7.887 kHz. SystemView Context parser, fixed-layout RT-Thread object validation, backend fallback behavior, GUI sanitization, and overlong-name gate are implemented. Device.flash reads multi-segment HEX/BIN regions back before reset, Device.reset sends cmd.reset_chip(), and all four regenerated cleanup artifacts contain targetVerified=true plus targetReset, zero controls, Tauri exit, and port release.

milestones.name: Offline download configurator
milestones.tests: Python 634 passed in 37.69s; Node release harness 51 passed; GUI 19 files / 246 tests passed in 18.16s; Vite 144 modules; Rust 6 passed and cargo check completed
milestones.evidence: V4.3.4 detected through cmd.get_version(); offline_download.py deployed with two ordered HEX files; load.offline() completed both downloads; independent contiguous Flash readback matched 19,296 bootloader bytes and 113,728 application bytes
milestones.review: full automated regression, diff validation, privacy scan, and real hardware closed loop completed
milestones.notes: V2/V3 always generate python/offline_download.py. V4 accepts a safe custom .py filename, requires screen selection confirmation before GUI trigger, and uses offline_download.py for unattended HIL. No whole-chip erase command is generated. Installed runtime detection, automatic preview/deploy, and trigger reuse the connected Device bridge instead of opening the CDC port twice.

milestones.name: RTT dashboard runtime-state repair
milestones.tests: Python 635 passed in 27.33s; Node release harness 51 passed; GUI 19 files / 249 tests passed in 17.38s; Vite 144 modules; Rust 6 passed; focused RTT Worker/View 72 passed
milestones.evidence: Rebuilt installed NSIS restored the project and connected with AXF symbols. Initial HIL received ordered RTT raw and four-channel waveform payloads with zero drops. After the rendering correction, real Edge visible-window HIL processed 93 data frames and 20,093 items in 10 seconds with 37 Canvas clears and 148 strokes, zero frontend sequence errors, zero transport/backend drops, pause with zero draws, resume with 20 clears and 80 strokes, complete resource cleanup, and RAM fixture dearm.
milestones.review: focused RED/GREEN regression, full automated regression, production build, bundle install, sanitized HIL, diff validation, and generated-artifact cleanup completed
milestones.notes: RTT start now returns a success boolean to the component, so a failed control-plane start cannot create an orphan binary WebSocket. The manager detects Device ERROR, stops, releases its API lease through the failure callback, exposes status.error, and the RTT toolbar stops the binary client and displays the runtime error. The numeric Canvas remains hidden until numeric RTT data arrives. The Worker includes bufferStartMs/bufferEndMs in numeric batches and RTT View requests envelopes over that finite range, preventing all samples from collapsing into one pixel and one invisible point per constant channel. Unsigned rebuilt NSIS SHA-256 F7C84453687ABCEF669CAA36EAEC75CA5A42ED12124119FCAF9433312D9C709E; MSI SHA-256 134B3B426A3F6FFA19440FD755BF196A3664F9AA18BC40083E11C78301307676.

milestones.name: RTT named waveform and smooth refresh
milestones.tests: Python 636 passed in 27.32s; GUI 19 files / 249 tests passed in 16.97s; Vite 144 modules; Rust 6 passed; focused RTT Python 25 and GUI 13 passed; Keil App build 0 errors and 0 warnings
milestones.evidence: Bootloader-preserving App HEX covered 0x08005000 through 0x08020C97 and passed Flash readback verification without whole-chip erase. Installed-runtime HIL recognized speed,temp after attaching to an already-running stream, delivered more than 3,000 parsed points in three seconds with zero drops, and rendered two changing named curves. A five-second Edge gate measured 119 Canvas clears, 238 strokes, 23.8 FPS, about 25 ms log update intervals, zero draws while paused, 29 draws in 1.2 seconds after resume, and clean stop.
milestones.review: root-cause analysis, focused and full regression, production build, NSIS/MSI bundle, installed sidecar smoke, real Edge Worker/Canvas gate, diff validation, privacy-safe memory update, and generated-artifact cleanup completed
milestones.notes: RTT delivery uses 50 Hz read windows and flushes incomplete numeric batches each cycle. The bridge polling quantum is 10 ms. The virtual log flush interval is 33 ms. Two consecutive matching numeric layouts are required before channel lock, preventing a mid-line first chunk such as peed=90 from replacing speed. Unsigned rebuilt NSIS SHA-256 E6D8B4A695FDEE8C56F331D6055A6E0B6D43C2B8605216934EE848EEE34E30EA; MSI SHA-256 CA0234FABED078F7242CE1AE3F0D060C76A38D416B4F51F949813E2FD013A5E9.

milestones.name: Installed GUI ordinary-user experience recovery
milestones.tests: GUI 20 files / 252 tests passed; Vite transformed 144 modules; focused RED/GREEN tests cover structured offline conflicts, nonblocking invalid-symbol feedback, and direct stop behavior
milestones.evidence: Computer Use completed real installed-app connection, RTT, RTOS Trace, memory, symbol, and SuperWatch interactions. Edge/Playwright reproduced the resource-conflict chain and verified the fixes. A fresh unsigned NSIS bundle installed with exit 0; the installed application restored the project, connected to the real target, displayed the invalid-symbol error inline without a dialog, stopped SuperWatch directly, released the bridge, and detected V4.3.4 on the offline page.
milestones.review: root-cause tracing, focused RED/GREEN tests, full GUI regression, production build, NSIS build/install, installed Computer Use retest, privacy cleanup, and diff validation completed
milestones.notes: The original SuperWatch stop confirmation caused Computer Use click timeout and retained the bridge lease. Offline detect then received a structured PROBE_BUSY response, but the frontend discarded the object and showed only Conflict. Both ends of that user-visible chain are fixed.

milestones.name: Installed online-flash MicroKeen discovery and firmware auto-inspection
milestones.tests: GUI 20 files / 255 tests passed; online-flash GUI 48 passed; online-flash Python 96 passed; Rust 6 passed; Vite transformed 144 modules
milestones.evidence: Computer Use cold-started the rebuilt unsigned NSIS installation, entered Online Flash from another page, and observed MicroKeenV4 CMSIS-DAP automatically discovered and selected without pressing Refresh. A minimal App-address HEX was automatically inspected. A BIN remained blocked until an explicit App base address was entered, then automatically inspected. No erase, program, or whole-chip operation was started.
milestones.review: root-cause tracing, RED/GREEN transient-enumeration coverage, full regression, production and NSIS-only build, silent overwrite install, installed Computer Use retest, privacy cleanup, and diff validation completed
milestones.notes: All CMSIS-DAP descriptions containing MicroKeen remain allowed, covering V2, V3, V4, and future MicroKeen variants while unique_id distinguishes devices. The original installed sidecar omitted pyOCD parser data and cmsis_pack_manager native runtime files. After packaging those dependencies, page-entry discovery still stopped on the first cold-start 500 because only empty arrays were retried; discovery now retries bounded transient errors as well.

milestones.name: SuperWatch symbol workspace and reconnect-safe typed writes
milestones.tests: Python 659 passed; GUI 24 files / 273 tests passed; Rust 6 passed; focused SuperWatch transaction regression 4 passed; Vite production build and MSI/NSIS bundles completed
milestones.evidence: Computer Use verified the installed application after an explicit disconnect/reconnect cycle. The stable init_ok variable displayed 'verified: 0' after the typed write, no Failed to fetch error appeared, continuous dump acquisition resumed immediately, and backend read cycles increased by 21 in two seconds with zero read errors. Pause, resume, stop, buffer reset, symbol reparse preservation, X/Y zoom, and drag pan also passed. Online Flash automatically discovered the MicroKeen probe, automatically inspected the App HEX, programmed only the embedded App range, and completed connect, erase, program, verify, reset, and disconnect without whole-chip erase. The matching BIN required an explicit App base and then inspected automatically.
milestones.review: ordinary-user Computer Use exploration, root-cause tracing, focused RED/GREEN tests, two rebuilt installer overwrite cycles, real target retest, full automated regression, privacy scan, generated-artifact cleanup, and diff validation completed
milestones.notes: The first failure had two layers. SuperWatchRuntime survived reconnect but prepare() returned before rebinding the current Device, so writes used a closed Device while polling used the new Device. After rebinding, one-shot dump_memory readback could leave firmware unable to restart continuous dump; command-mode read_memory verification avoids that stream transition and restores acquisition reliably.

milestones.name: SuperWatch waveform visibility and shared-axis workspace
milestones.tests: Python 659 passed in 37.95s; GUI 24 files / 279 tests passed; focused SuperWatch tests 55 passed; WaveformViewer 48 passed in isolation; Vite transformed 1906 modules; Rust 6 passed and cargo check completed
milestones.evidence: Computer Use verified the installed application with two changing float variables. The redundant right Watch table was absent and the waveform filled the right workspace. Hiding one selected variable through its eye control removed only that curve while its displayed value continued changing; restoring the eye brought the curve back. Six shared Y-axis tick values were visible. Y wheel zoom changed the range, drag shifted its center, and double-click restored the default range. Pause froze the visible point count while the transport buffer continued growing, resume advanced the curve to the current buffer, stop cleared the backend buffer to zero, Alt+F4 closed the application, and the sidecar, port, and development server resources were released.
milestones.review: design refinement, implementation plan, focused component regression, full Python/GUI/Rust regression, production build, installed Computer Use HIL, privacy-safe memory update, diff validation, and generated-artifact cleanup completed
milestones.notes: The first full GUI run shared the host with Python and Rust and exceeded the synthetic VOFA peak-heap threshold by about 15 MiB while all functional assertions passed. The complete WaveformViewer file and the full GUI suite both passed when rerun without competing toolchains, identifying parallel host memory pressure rather than a stable product regression.

Four regenerated cleanup artifacts independently prove target reflash, actual Flash readback, reset, zero controls, Tauri exit, and API/CDP port release.

Root causes fixed at protocol, RAM fallback, backend lifecycle, and GUI display boundaries. Focused Python 50 and GUI 8 tests pass. Fresh packaged HIL ran 20.657 seconds with 414568 events, zero parser drops, one valid protocol task name, zero invalid names, and complete cleanup.
Python 634 passed in 37.69s; Node release harness 51 passed; GUI 19 files / 246 tests passed in 18.16s; Vite transformed 144 modules; Rust 6 passed and cargo check completed.

verification.area: Task 11 online-flash safety and installer lifecycle
verification.result: Real App programming used 56 covered 2 KiB sectors and preserved independently verified bootloader and App regions. Final 56f1df6 NSIS/MSI bundles install and uninstall with exit 0, run without Python on PATH or Python descendants, and release ports. Final installed NSIS App HEX verify succeeded read-only; expected VERIFY_FAIL reported first mismatch 0x08000000. NSIS uninstall preserved both Pack/user cache fingerprints.

The large black area was the empty virtual text log joined visually to the 160 px numeric Canvas. The text log now remains mounted but hidden until text arrives, so first-batch reception is preserved; installed DOM measured zero empty-log width/height and full GUI tests pass.

21 payload assets and 18 sanitized rc1 JSON files were copied to the external release directory. All sizes and SHA-256 values match the manifest; checksum lines are sorted by asset name. The complete Pack-index, uncached GigaDevice DFP 2.2.1 install, restart reuse, and final installed-runtime cache reuse are mapped into the report and rc1-pack-catalog-cache.json.

GitHub prerelease v0.1.0-rc.1 is non-draft and contains 23 assets. All assets were downloaded to an isolated temporary directory and matched local name, byte size, and SHA-256; the temporary verification copy was removed. Tag and manifest source both resolve to 1c7c5aac3f49f95d4195a0f07eed51bdaf6dcde6.

cmd.get_version() identified V4.3.4. The structured deploy API copied an ordered bootloader HEX and application HEX plus python/offline_download.py to the MKLink disk, then load.offline() reported both images loaded successfully and auto download finished. Independent readback matched both complete configured ranges byte for byte, preserving the bootloader and application together without whole-chip erase. A real API reproduction also connected Device first and then detected V4.3.4 through the same bridge, covering installed-runtime project restoration.

verification.area: RTT dashboard failure and installed HIL
verification.result: Python 635, Node 51, GUI 249, and Rust 6 tests passed; Vite production build transformed 144 modules. The rebuilt installed application restored the project and loaded AXF symbols. Real Edge HIL confirmed ordered RTT raw plus four-channel waveform delivery, 37 Canvas clears and 148 strokes in the visible interval, zero measured loss, pause/resume rendering behavior, clean stop, zero clients, released resources, and target dearm. Runtime Device ERROR is terminal and visible instead of remaining falsely running.

verification.area: RTT named waveform and smooth refresh HIL
verification.result: Python 636, GUI 249, and Rust 6 tests passed; Vite transformed 144 modules; Keil App build had 0 errors and 0 warnings. The App-only HEX began at 0x08005000, passed readback verification, and preserved the Bootloader without whole-chip erase. Installed HIL recognized speed,temp during mid-line attachment, delivered over 1,000 parsed points/s with zero drops, rendered changing curves at 23.8 FPS, refreshed the virtual log at roughly 25 ms intervals, paused with zero draws, resumed normally, and stopped cleanly.

Computer Use verified real connection/disconnection, RTT named data and curves, pause/resume/stop, RTOS Trace collection and stop, memory read, symbol search, SuperWatch symbol selection, error recovery, and navigation. Edge/Playwright continued after the Windows capture channel failed. GUI 252 tests and Vite 144 modules passed. The rebuilt NSIS installed with exit 0 and the installed application passed the corrected invalid-symbol, direct-stop, resource-release, and offline V4.3.4 detection flows.

The rebuilt NSIS installed with exit 0. Computer Use verified automatic MicroKeenV4 CMSIS-DAP discovery/selection, no manual firmware-inspection button, automatic HEX inspection, explicit BIN base validation, and automatic BIN inspection after entering an App address. A later App-only HEX program used the embedded range beginning at 0x08005000 and completed connect, covered-sector erase, program, verify, reset, and disconnect without whole-chip erase or Bootloader coverage.

verification.area: SuperWatch reconnect and typed-write installed HIL
verification.result: Python 659, GUI 273, and Rust 6 tests passed. Two full MSI/NSIS bundles built and the NSIS installer overwrote the installed application with exit 0. Computer Use verified disconnect/reconnect, stable uint8 typed write with verified readback, immediate continuous dump recovery, 21 additional read cycles in two seconds, zero read errors, pause/resume, clean stop, and buffer reset to zero.

verification.area: SuperWatch waveform visibility and shared-axis installed HIL
verification.result: Python 659, GUI 279, focused SuperWatch 55, isolated WaveformViewer 48, and Rust 6 tests passed; cargo check and the Vite production build also passed. Computer Use verified the expanded waveform workspace, independent eye visibility with continued acquisition, shared numeric Y ticks, Y zoom/pan/reset, pause with continued buffering, resume catch-up, stop with backend buffer reset, normal application exit, and sidecar/port release.

decisions: Only MKLink-exposed CMSIS-DAP is supported by the online flash UI.
BIN requires an explicit base address; HEX uses embedded mappings.
CMSIS-Pack catalog is complete but DFP files are downloaded on demand and never committed or bundled.
High-rate data uses a versioned binary WebSocket data plane; REST/SSE remains for control and legacy low-rate paths.
Acquisition must never wait for a browser; each client has a bounded queue that drops the oldest batch with explicit telemetry.
Rendering is decoupled from acquisition and capped at 30 FPS with min/max pixel-envelope decimation.

Ordinary online-flash program is enforced at both GUI and API boundaries: the inspected image is target-bound, reliable FLM coverage is recomputed, and erase sectors must exactly equal image-covered sectors; whole-chip erase remains a separate explicit confirmation.
Offline probe model selection is derived from cmd.get_version(). V2/V3 deploy python/offline_download.py; V4 may deploy a safe custom .py name selected on the probe screen.
Offline scripts preserve firmware order, use embedded HEX addresses, require BIN base addresses, and never generate a whole-chip erase operation.
The GUI trigger is disabled until the current configuration is deployed; V4 custom scripts require explicit confirmation that the same script is selected on the probe screen.
RTT View hides the empty text terminal but retains the numeric Canvas, which appears only when numeric channels are available.

RTT binary transport starts only after a successful control-plane start. A runtime Device ERROR is terminal, releases the dashboard lease, is exposed as status.error, and stops the frontend binary client.

RTT waveform envelopes use the Worker's finite buffered timestamp range. Requesting 0..Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER is forbidden because it collapses real-time samples into one pixel and makes constant channels invisible.
RTT dashboard delivery uses 50 Hz read windows, flushes partial raw and numeric batches each cycle, and keeps Canvas rendering capped by the shared 30 FPS scheduler.
RTT numeric channel names are locked only after two consecutive rows expose the same layout; the first stream chunk may begin in the middle of a line.
SuperWatch typed writes serialize against acquisition: stop continuous dump, write through flush_memory, verify with command-mode read_memory, then restore the prior running or paused state.
SuperWatch prepare always rebinds the current Device on reconnect even when the symbol runtime and selected watch list are preserved.

SuperWatch variable checkboxes control acquisition while adjacent eye controls affect rendering only; hidden curves keep sampling and updating their current values. The desktop waveform uses the full right workspace and a shared numeric Y axis with wheel zoom, drag pan, and double-click reset.
Subagent workflow is implementer, spec review, then quality review; review fixes receive new commits and are re-reviewed.

hardware.probe: MKLink V4 connected; identifier intentionally omitted
hardware.online_flash_project: STM32F103 bootloader fixture; local path intentionally omitted
hardware.stream_hil_project: STM32F103 application fixture; local path intentionally omitted
hardware.keil_root: local Keil installation; path intentionally omitted
hardware.permission: User permits adding test firmware code, compiling with Keil, flashing, and testing VOFA/SystemView/variable reads.

known_limits: Physical target power loss, probe unplug, SWD wire disconnect, second non-MKLink probe comparison, and physical network disconnect were not executed.

Only RTT retained a measured overload boundary. SystemView pairs=2/tick and VOFA/SuperWatch at the fastest supported 10 us request are qualified stable zero-drop rates, not claimed drop-bounded maxima.
Real hidden-tab HIL remains not established: Edge 130 rejected the visibility override and a background tab did not set document.hidden=true; deterministic hidden scheduling tests pass.
GUI npm audit reports two pre-existing high vulnerabilities; dependencies were not changed during protocol work.
Task 3 uses the first CONTROL heartbeat as a subscription-ready barrier in tests because batches published before subscribe are intentionally not cached.
The Windows RC executables and installers are unsigned and may show an unknown-publisher warning.

The first final-package restricted-environment teardown omitted SystemDrive in the test harness, so Windows created a literal test-cache directory after product processes and ports exited; the cache was removed and the corrected NSIS lifecycle rerun passed completely.

Task 11 did not reflash the whole-chip stream fixture because the target currently uses a bootloader-preserving App layout; RTT performance remains supported by the existing 600-second packaged HIL artifact.
V2/V3 offline deployment is covered by automated tests but was not physically executed in this session; V4.3.4 is the only newly qualified offline hardware model.

Windows computer-use visual automation was unavailable because its native communication pipe was missing; GUI behavior was verified by 249 Vitest tests, the production build, direct installed FastAPI/WebSocket HIL, and a real Edge Worker/Canvas stroke gate. No new Windows screenshot evidence is claimed.

Computer Use was available for most of the ordinary-user session, then its window capture channel twice timed out with FrameArrived errors. It recovered after the rebuilt installer was installed and completed the final installed-app retest. Screenshots remain local test evidence only and were deleted before commit.

Installed Online Flash discovery and App-only programming were physically qualified with MicroKeenV4 only. V2 and V3 share the MicroKeen CMSIS-DAP description policy but were not physically available for this pass.
Serial and Modbus physical fixtures were not established in this ordinary-user pass and remain NOT ESTABLISHED.

The first installed Offline Flash page load once showed the English transient error 'online flash operation failed'; pressing Search Target immediately succeeded. Reproduction and startup-order root cause remain open.

Immediately after the large PyInstaller/Rust build, Windows displayed one crashrpt.exe 0xc000012d dialog over the installed app. Dismissing it allowed normal operation; it was not attributed to Mklink without further reproduction.
Do not expose full probe IDs, COM ports, credentials, user names, raw logs, screenshots, Pack files, or build artifacts in Git.
next_actions: Reproduce and fix the first-load Offline Flash target-search transient that can display 'online flash operation failed' before a manual retry succeeds.

Collect tester feedback for the RTT named temp/speed curves, SuperWatch symbol workspace, typed writes, eye visibility, shared-axis navigation, offline configurator, and the v0.1.0-rc.1 GitHub prerelease.
Qualify V2 and V3 physical offline deployment when those probe models are available; automated script-generation coverage already passes.
For the next release, add code signing before promoting beyond prerelease.
Keep hidden-document, Serial, Modbus, and physical fault-injection results NOT ESTABLISHED unless their required runtime or fixture is actually present.
continuation_protocol: Read: docs/ai/CURRENT_HANDOFF.md and the active plan
Run: git status --short and git log -12 --oneline; reconcile with repository.head
Resume current_task before starting later tasks
Before ending: update project-memory.json, run render, validate, tests proportional to changes, git diff --check, commit, and push
